rationale: Load-bearing TokenKey gateway/service injections that intentionally live in upstream-shaped hotspot files. These touches are small and compile-clean if dropped, so upstream merges can silently revert them unless preflight and upstream-merge PR shape check their literal hooks.

sentinels.rationale: Code Assist 429 fallback-model protection depends on exactly two upstream-file hooks: forwarding stores the resolved upstream Gemini model in context, and handleGeminiUpstreamError delegates to the TK companion before account-level SetRateLimited. Dropping either hook brings back whole-account cooldowns for single-model capacity errors.
TK companion that keeps Gemini Code Assist MODEL_CAPACITY_EXHAUSTED scoped to the affected upstream model, including the production case where Google omits ErrorInfo.metadata.model.

Scheduling must read model_rate_limits through the same Gemini group-level Claude→Gemini mapping used by forwarding; otherwise writes use gemini-* keys while subsequent /v1/messages reads probe claude-* keys.
Focused regression proving Gemini dispatch mapping affects model-level rate-limit reads.
Focused regressions proving model-scoped Code Assist 429s without metadata.model do not fall back to account-level cooldown.
